generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance BuildingTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded sensor in building systems providing real-time temperature readings via Eclipse Hono/Ditto for low-latency synchronization with the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedData BuildingTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance BuildingOccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Occupancy detection sensor integrated into building infrastructure, transmitting occupancy patterns via Eclipse Hono/Ditto for real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData BuildingOccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralIntegritySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Structural health monitoring sensor embedded in building elements (e.g., beams, columns) transmitting vibration and stress data via Eclipse Hono/Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralIntegrityDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionMeter : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Smart meter measuring real-time energy consumption across building subsystems (HVAC, lighting, etc.) with Eclipse Hono/Ditto synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

instance HVACPerformanceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "HVAC system sensor monitoring airflow, pressure, and efficiency metrics with event-driven transmission via Eclipse Hono/Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HVACPerformanceDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance BuildingTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BuildingTemperatureSensor
]

instance BuildingOccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BuildingOccupancySensor
]

instance StructuralIntegrityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralIntegritySensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionMeter
]

instance HVACPerformanceDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACPerformanceSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BuildingInformationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically rich BIM model encapsulating geometric, spatial, and functional characteristics of building components (IFC or proprietary formats) serving as the static backbone for the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler, UnrealEngineEnabler
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated real-time data streams from IoT sensors and control systems synchronized via Eclipse Hono/Ditto, representing dynamic physical asset states."
    DTDFVocab:fromData BuildingTemperatureDataTransmitted, BuildingOccupancyDataTransmitted, StructuralIntegrityDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted, HVACPerformanceDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance HistoricalPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Longitudinal data repository capturing building performance metrics (energy, structural, occupancy) for predictive analytics and trend analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight messaging protocol enabling scalable, low-latency communication between IoT devices and the Digital Twin in heterogeneous environments."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance EclipseDittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital Twin framework providing device management, state modeling, and event-driven interactions to maintain consistent virtual representations of physical assets."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationAndAnalysisService, AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance UnrealEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-fidelity visualization engine enabling interactive 3D rendering of the Digital Twin for immersive analysis and stakeholder collaboration."
    DTDFVocab:enables InteractiveVisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous observation of building performance metrics (structural integrity, energy consumption, occupancy) via integrated runtime data streams."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingPerformanceInsight, SystemStateInsight
]

instance SimulationAndAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Lifecycle-encompassing optimization through virtual testing of design alternatives, operational scenarios, and refurbishment strategies."
    DTDFVocab:provides DesignOptimizationInsight, OperationalScenarioInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data-driven decision-making by forecasting asset degradation, maintenance needs, and adaptive requirements based on historical and real-time data."
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceForecastInsight, AdaptiveRequirementInsight
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Rich, immersive 3D representations of the Digital Twin enabling stakeholders to explore building systems intuitively for collaborative decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:provides ImmersiveBuildingViewInsight, ScenarioExplorationInsight
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time synchronization highlighting discrepancies between physical and digital states to identify operational anomalies."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyAlertAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuously updated metrics on structural integrity, energy consumption, and occupancy patterns derived from real-time sensor data."
]

instance SystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comprehensive overview of current building system states (HVAC, electrical, mechanical) for operational monitoring."
]

instance DesignOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Virtual testing results comparing design alternatives to identify optimal configurations for building performance."
]

instance OperationalScenarioInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulated outcomes of various operational scenarios (e.g., energy-saving modes, occupancy adjustments) to inform decision-making."
]

instance MaintenanceForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance recommendations based on asset degradation trends and historical performance data."
]

instance AdaptiveRequirementInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecasted adaptive requirements (e.g., refurbishment needs, regulatory compliance updates) derived from long-term data analysis."
]

instance ImmersiveBuildingViewInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization of building systems with real-time data overlays for intuitive exploration."
]

instance ScenarioExplorationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Virtual walkthroughs of proposed building modifications or operational changes before physical implementation."
]

instance AnomalyAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated notification system triggering alerts for critical deviations between physical and digital twin states."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HVACControllerActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building control system actuator for HVAC systems, integrated into BIM environment to enable real-time operational adjustments via Eclipse Hono/Ditto bidirectional communication."
]

instance LightingSystemActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Automated lighting control actuator with semantic integration into the Digital Twin for energy-efficient operational management."
]

instance StructuralAdjustmentActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator for adaptive structural systems (e.g., dampers, tensioning systems) enabling real-time adjustments based on Digital Twin insights."
]

// C1: Environment
instance AECOBuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Heterogeneous cyber-physical infrastructure asset ecosystem spanning the entire building lifecycle from design to operations."
    base:contains BuildingInformationModel, RealTimeOperationalData, HistoricalPerformanceData,
                 HVACControllerActuator, LightingSystemActuator, StructuralAdjustmentActuator
]

instance AECOBuildingEnvironmentComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component within AECO building environment"
    base:isContainedIn AECOBuildingEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT system enables **control flows from digital to physical** through the interaction of **Facility Managers**, who operate the deployed DT for real-time monitoring and control of physical infrastructure. The framework facilitates **bidirectional data exchange**, allowing the DT to send control signals back to the Building Control System (BCS) or other physical systems. While the document does not specify exact command types (e.g., HVAC setpoints) or control message formats, it emphasizes the **holistic approach to building life cycle management** via the DT Framework. Validation and authentication mechanisms are not detailed, but the system’s **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** include **interoperability** and **scalability**, suggesting robust integration with heterogeneous systems. Feedback systems are implied by the **real-time monitoring and control** loop, though specifics (e.g., confirmation protocols) are absent. Conflict resolution and command execution confirmation are not explicitly described, but the **runtime configuration management** capability hints at dynamic adjustments to mitigate conflicts during operation."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure **continuous synchronization** between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, enabling near-instantaneous bidirectional updates. While the document does not specify exact synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms, or event-driven triggers), the requirement implies **low-latency operational constraints** to support dynamic monitoring and control by *Facility Managers*. The system supports **runtime configuration management**, allowing adjustments without full redeployment, which suggests **adaptive temporal granularity** for different services (e.g., high-frequency sensor data for control systems vs. periodic analytics updates). Latency requirements are implicitly tied to **interactive visualization** and **real-time monitoring**, though no explicit thresholds (e.g., <100ms) are provided. The framework’s modular architecture enables **service-specific time scales**, where critical operational services (e.g., HVAC control) may demand stricter temporal alignment than non-critical analytics modules. The absence of explicit time-scale specifications in the document suggests a **flexible, demand-driven approach** prioritizing synchronization over rigid periodic updates, aligning with the AECO domain’s need for dynamic operational responsiveness."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **coordinated, role-based constellation architecture**, where the *DT Framework* serves as the **central orchestrator** integrating **enriched BIM models** into **operational Digital Twins**. The architecture follows a **modular, service-oriented pattern**, with:
  - **Component Integration**: Standardized extension points enable **plug-and-play service integration**, allowing *DT Engineers* to add custom analytics or control modules without redeploying the entire system.
  - **Resource Management**: The framework manages **runtime configuration**, dynamically adjusting system behavior to operational needs (e.g., prioritizing real-time sensor data for critical services).
  - **System-Wide Coordination**: Bidirectional data exchange between physical buildings and digital counterparts is facilitated by the central hub, ensuring **holistic life cycle management**. The **life cycle roles** (*Building Control Engineers*, *Facility Managers*, *DT Engineers*) define **scope-specific orchestration**, where:
    - *Building Control Engineers* initiate deployment via BIM tools (device modeling).
    - *Facility Managers* interact with deployed twins for monitoring/control.
    - *DT Engineers* extend capabilities through service integration.
  The constellation leverages **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** to address **interoperability** (cross-system coordination), **scalability** (from components to infrastructure), and **maintainability** (low-overhead updates). The absence of explicit patterns (e.g., microservices, event-driven) suggests a **hybrid approach**, prioritizing **modularity** and **runtime adaptability** over rigid architectural prescriptions."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
  **Life Cycle Stages of the Digital Twin (DT):**
  The document outlines a **model-driven DT deployment framework** that addresses the **complete life cycle of DT deployment and operation**, spanning from initial device modeling to the development of custom DT services. Key phases include:

  1. **Device Modeling & BIM Integration**
     - **Representation Type:** Enriched BIM models with **Building Control System (BCS) components** (sensors, actuators, controllers).
     - **Stakeholder Role:** *Building Control Engineers* define devices and control logic.
     - **Transition:** Manual configuration in BIM, followed by **automated extraction** of structured representations via **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)** principles.

  2. **DT Deployment & Artifact Generation**
     - **Representation Type:** Structured, deployable DT artifacts (e.g., executable models, service definitions).
     - **Transition:** Automated generation from enriched BIM models with **minimal manual intervention**.
     - **Governance:** Modular architecture with **standardized extension points** for custom services.

  3. **Operation & Real-Time Synchronization**
     - **Representation Type:** Dynamic, real-time synchronization between **physical infrastructure** and **digital twin**.
     - **Stakeholder Role:** *Facility Managers* monitor system states and interact with the twin.
     - **Transition:** Continuous data exchange ensures **bidirectional synchronization**.

  4. **Customization & Evolution**
     - **Representation Type:** Extensible DT with **custom services/plugins** (e.g., analytics, predictive maintenance).
     - **Stakeholder Role:** *DT Engineers* develop and integrate extensions.
     - **Governance:** Modular design allows **runtime evolution** to adapt to changing operational needs.

  **Lifecycle Governance Approaches:**
  - **Modular Architecture:** Standardized extension points ensure **interoperability** and **scalability**.
  - **Automation:** MDE principles reduce manual effort in transitions between phases.
  - **Stakeholder-Specific Workflows:** Roles (Building Control Engineer, Facility Manager, DT Engineer) are aligned with distinct phases.
  - **Real-Time Feedback Loops:** Continuous synchronization supports **adaptive governance** during operation.
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system prioritizes **semantic integration** and **real-time connectivity** to ensure high fidelity between physical assets and their digital representations. Validation and verification methods are implied through the **model-driven automation framework**, which aligns design-time BIM models with runtime operations, reducing discrepancies via **automated deployment (R2)** and **live synchronization (R3)**. Uncertainty sources may include **data latency** (due to IoT transmission delays) or **model abstraction gaps** (e.g., simplifications in BIM vs. real-world dynamics), though no explicit quantification or error correction mechanisms are described. Quality assurance processes are inferred from the **Design Science Research (DSR) methodology**, which emphasizes **prototyping and experimentation (Section V)** to iteratively refine model accuracy. The system’s **modular architecture (R4)** supports extensibility for incorporating advanced validation techniques (e.g., machine learning for anomaly detection), but no specific protocols (e.g., calibration procedures, tolerance thresholds) are documented."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT system employs a **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**, enabling seamless integration of custom services and analytics. The framework supports **interoperability across heterogeneous systems**, as outlined in the non-functional requirements (NF_X), ensuring compatibility with diverse operational environments. While the document does not explicitly detail specific communication protocols (e.g., OPC UA, Modbus), it references **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** as technologies enabling real-time data exchange and synchronization. The network topology is not explicitly described, but the **modular and service-oriented design** suggests a flexible, potentially hybrid architecture. Bandwidth and latency requirements are implied by the **real-time synchronization** need but are not quantified. Reliability and redundancy mechanisms are addressed under **non-functional requirements**, focusing on **scalability** and **maintainability**, though specifics (e.g., failover protocols) are not provided. Security protocols for network communication are not detailed, but the emphasis on **interoperability** and **accessibility** for varied technical expertise implies adherence to industry standards."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT system is deployed as a **centralized DT Framework**, acting as a hub for bidirectional data exchange between enriched BIM models and operational Digital Twins. The framework supports **interactive visualization** (e.g., via tools like Unreal Engine, inferred from the context of immersive interfaces) and is designed for **low-overhead updates**, enabling runtime configuration adjustments without full redeployment. While the document does not explicitly specify hosting platforms (e.g., cloud, edge, or on-premise), the **scalability** and **maintainability** requirements suggest flexibility in deployment models. Computational and storage requirements are not detailed, but the system’s modularity implies support for **scalable resource allocation** from individual components to entire infrastructure systems. Availability and reliability assurances are addressed under **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, though specific mechanisms (e.g., redundant hosting platforms) are not described. The deployment process involves **Building Control Engineers** (for device modeling) and **DT Engineers** (for service integration), with **Facility Managers** interacting with the deployed DT for operational tasks."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** to enable seamless integration with external tools and platforms. It explicitly supports **bidirectional data exchange** with **BIM tools** (e.g., for device modeling and deployment) and facilitates interactions with **operational systems** used by Facility Managers. While no specific protocols (e.g., IFC, OPC UA, MQTT) or external systems (e.g., ERP, FMS) are mentioned, the framework’s **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points** imply compatibility with industry-standard interoperability mechanisms. Dependencies on external systems are managed through the central DT Framework hub, ensuring a cohesive integration pattern without explicit details on underlying protocols or enterprise system linkages."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework prioritizes **compatibility with existing AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) standards**, particularly **Building Information Modeling (BIM)** as the backbone for DT deployment. While the document acknowledges the **absence of standardized integration frameworks** for DTs, it emphasizes adherence to **BIM-based workflows** and **semantic interoperability** between enriched BIM models and DT artifacts. No specific industry standards (e.g., ISO 19650, OPC UA, IFC) or certification requirements are explicitly mentioned, though the framework’s modular design implies potential alignment with future interoperability standards. The focus is on **semantic consistency** through MDE-driven transformations and **modular extensibility**, ensuring compatibility with AECO-specific systems without rigid adherence to external protocols."
]
